metadata.description: Game development skill track with prerequisite ordering, colored by discipline

nodes.label: Game Mechanics
nodes.description: Core game mechanics: rules, goals, challenges, feedback loops. Mechanics vs dynamics vs aesthetics (MDA) framework.

nodes.label: Game Loops
nodes.description: Core game loop: input, update, render. Fixed vs variable timestep. Delta time. Frame rate independence. Game state management.

nodes.label: Level Design
nodes.description: Level layout principles, pacing, difficulty curves. Player guidance through environment. Gating mechanics. Playtesting levels.

nodes.label: Narrative Design
nodes.description: Storytelling in games: linear vs branching narratives. Environmental storytelling. Dialogue systems. Lore and world-building.

nodes.label: Player Psychology
nodes.description: Flow state and engagement. Motivation (intrinsic vs extrinsic). Bartle player types. Reward schedules. Difficulty and player skill.

nodes.label: Prototyping
nodes.description: Rapid prototyping techniques. Paper prototyping. Digital prototypes. Minimum viable product for games. Iterative design process.

nodes.label: Genre Analysis
nodes.description: Studying game genres: platformer, RPG, puzzle, strategy, roguelike. Genre conventions and player expectations. Genre blending.

nodes.label: Game Balance
nodes.description: Balancing game systems: resource economies, character stats, weapon damage. Spreadsheet modeling. Playtesting for balance.

nodes.label: Sprites & Sprite Sheets
nodes.description: Loading and displaying sprites. Sprite sheets and texture atlases. Sprite rendering order. Pixel art basics and resolution.

nodes.label: 2D Animation
nodes.description: Frame-by-frame animation. Animation controllers and state machines. Skeletal animation (Spine, DragonBones). Tweening and easing.

nodes.label: Collision Detection
nodes.description: AABB (axis-aligned bounding box) collision. Circle collision. Pixel-perfect collision. Collision layers and masks. Trigger zones.

nodes.label: Tilemaps
nodes.description: Tile-based level design. Tilemap editors and formats. Auto-tiling. Isometric and hexagonal tilemaps. Tilemap collision.

nodes.label: 2D Physics
nodes.description: Rigidbodies and colliders. Gravity, velocity, acceleration. Forces and impulses. Joints and constraints. Physics materials (bounce, friction).

nodes.label: Game UI (2D)
nodes.description: HUD elements: health bars, score, minimap. Menu systems: main menu, pause, settings. UI anchoring and scaling. Input handling for UI.

nodes.label: 2D Camera Systems
nodes.description: Camera follow and smoothing. Camera bounds and dead zones. Screen shake. Parallax scrolling backgrounds. Split-screen.

nodes.label: Input Handling
nodes.description: Keyboard, mouse, and gamepad input. Input mapping and rebinding. Touch input for mobile. Input buffering. Dead zones for analog sticks.

nodes.label: 3D Math
nodes.description: Vectors (2D/3D), dot product, cross product. Matrices for transformations. Quaternions for rotation. Coordinate systems and spaces.

nodes.label: 3D Meshes & Models
nodes.description: Vertices, edges, faces. Mesh formats (FBX, OBJ, glTF). Importing 3D models. LOD (level of detail). Mesh optimization.

nodes.label: Textures & Materials
nodes.description: UV mapping, texture coordinates. Diffuse, normal, specular maps. PBR materials (albedo, metallic, roughness). Texture atlases.

nodes.label: Lighting
nodes.description: Light types: directional, point, spot. Ambient lighting. Real-time vs baked lighting. Shadows: shadow maps, cascaded shadows. Global illumination.

nodes.label: 3D Cameras
nodes.description: Perspective vs orthographic projection. First-person, third-person, and orbital cameras. Camera collision. Cinemachine / virtual cameras.

nodes.label: Shaders
nodes.description: Vertex and fragment shaders. Shader languages (HLSL, GLSL). Shader Graph / visual shader editors. Common effects: toon, dissolve, water.

nodes.label: 3D Animation
nodes.description: Skeletal animation, rigging, skinning. Animation blending and layers. Inverse kinematics. Root motion. Animation state machines.

nodes.label: 3D Physics
nodes.description: 3D rigidbodies and colliders (box, sphere, capsule, mesh). Raycasting. Physics layers. Character controllers. Vehicle physics basics.

nodes.label: Unity: Scenes & Hierarchy
nodes.description: Unity Editor layout. Scenes, hierarchy, and inspector. Scene management and loading. Prefabs. Project organization and folder structure.

nodes.label: Unity: GameObjects & Components
nodes.description: GameObject-Component architecture. Transform component. Adding/removing components. Tags and layers. Enabling/disabling objects.

nodes.label: Unity: C# Scripting
nodes.description: MonoBehaviour lifecycle: Awake, Start, Update, FixedUpdate. GetComponent, Find. Coroutines. SerializeField and Inspector values. ScriptableObjects.

nodes.label: Unity: Physics System
nodes.description: Unity Rigidbody and Collider components. OnCollision and OnTrigger events. Physics materials. Raycasting in Unity. Layer-based collision.

nodes.label: Unity: UI System
nodes.description: Unity UI (uGUI): Canvas, panels, buttons, text. UI Toolkit basics. Event system. Anchoring and responsive layouts. UI animations.

nodes.label: Unity: Animation System
nodes.description: Animator Controller, animation clips. Blend trees. Animation events. Avatar and humanoid rig. Timeline for cutscenes.

nodes.label: Unity: Audio System
nodes.description: AudioSource and AudioListener. Audio clips and mixers. 3D spatial audio. Audio triggers. Background music management.

nodes.label: Unity: Particle System
nodes.description: Particle System and VFX Graph. Emission, shape, lifetime modules. Sub-emitters. Particle collision. Common effects: fire, smoke, sparks.

nodes.label: State Machines
nodes.description: Finite state machines for game logic. States, transitions, conditions. Enemy AI states. Player state management. Hierarchical state machines.

nodes.label: Pathfinding
nodes.description: A* algorithm. Navigation meshes (NavMesh). Waypoint systems. Obstacle avoidance. Dynamic pathfinding. Grid-based vs mesh-based navigation.

nodes.label: AI Behaviors
nodes.description: Behavior trees. Steering behaviors (seek, flee, wander). Perception systems (sight, hearing). Decision making. Enemy AI patterns.

nodes.label: Multiplayer Basics
nodes.description: Client-server vs peer-to-peer architecture. Lobbies and matchmaking. Player synchronization. Authoritative servers. Local multiplayer.

nodes.label: Game Networking
nodes.description: TCP vs UDP for games. Latency compensation: prediction, interpolation, lag compensation. Network serialization. Anti-cheat basics.

nodes.label: Save Systems
nodes.description: Serialization for game saves. Save file formats (JSON, binary). Auto-save and checkpoints. Cloud saves. Save data migration.

nodes.label: Object Pooling & Optimization
nodes.description: Object pooling for performance. Memory management. Draw call batching. Profiling and bottleneck identification. LOD and culling.

nodes.label: Event Systems
nodes.description: Observer pattern for game events. Event buses. Decoupling systems with events. Signal/slot patterns. Publish-subscribe in games.

nodes.label: Sound Effects
nodes.description: Sound effect sourcing and creation. Foley basics. Audio file formats and compression. Positional audio. Sound effect variation and randomization.

nodes.label: Game Music
nodes.description: Music loops and layers. Adaptive/dynamic music systems. Music transitions between scenes. Licensing and royalty-free music. FMOD/Wwise basics.

nodes.label: Particle Effects
nodes.description: Particle systems for visual feedback. Emitter shapes and behaviors. Particle textures and colors. Common effects: explosions, trails, magic.

nodes.label: Screen Shake & Feedback
nodes.description: Camera shake for impact. Hitstop/freeze frames. Haptic feedback. Visual damage indicators. Knockback and recoil effects.

nodes.label: Game Juice & Feel
nodes.description: Making games feel good: squash and stretch, anticipation, follow-through. Easing functions. Tweening. Responsive controls. Satisfying feedback loops.

nodes.label: Post-Processing
nodes.description: Post-processing effects: bloom, vignette, color grading, depth of field. Anti-aliasing. Screen-space reflections. Tonemapping.

nodes.label: Target Platforms
nodes.description: Platform considerations: PC, console, mobile, web. Platform-specific requirements. Build settings. Performance targets per platform.

nodes.label: Monetization
nodes.description: Revenue models: premium, free-to-play, ads, DLC, subscriptions. In-app purchases. Ethical monetization. Pricing strategies.

nodes.label: Game Marketing
nodes.description: Steam page optimization. Trailers and screenshots. Social media presence. Press outreach. Wishlists and community building. Launch timing.

nodes.label: Game Jams
nodes.description: Participating in game jams (Ludum Dare, GMTK). Scoping for a jam. Rapid development workflow. Post-jam polish and iteration.

nodes.label: Portfolio & Career
nodes.description: Building a game dev portfolio. Itch.io and portfolio websites. Documenting your process. Networking in the industry. Career paths in game dev.

nodes.label: Store Publishing
nodes.description: Publishing to Steam, App Store, Google Play, itch.io. Store requirements and guidelines. Age ratings. Storefront assets and descriptions.

nodes.label: QA & Playtesting
nodes.description: Bug tracking and reporting. Playtesting methodology. Alpha and beta testing. Automated testing for games. Regression testing.

nodes.label: Player Movement
nodes.description: Character controllers for 2D and 3D. Platformer movement (jump, dash, wall slide). Top-down and side-scrolling movement. Coyote time and input buffering.

nodes.label: Procedural Generation
nodes.description: Random level generation algorithms. Perlin/simplex noise. Wave function collapse. Procedural content: dungeons, terrain, items. Seeded randomness.

nodes.label: Inventory Systems
nodes.description: Item data structures and databases. Inventory UI: grid, list, weight-based. Item stacking, sorting, filtering. Equipment and slot systems.

nodes.label: Dialogue Systems
nodes.description: Dialogue trees and branching conversations. Dialogue markup formats (Yarn, Ink). Typewriter text effects. Choice-consequence tracking.

nodes.label: Combat Systems
nodes.description: Real-time vs turn-based combat. Hit detection and hitboxes. Damage calculation. Combo systems. Health and damage feedback.

nodes.label: Terrain & Environment
nodes.description: Heightmap-based terrain. Terrain texturing and splatmaps. Vegetation and detail objects. Terrain LOD. Environmental storytelling in 3D.

nodes.label: Rendering Pipeline
nodes.description: Forward vs deferred rendering. Render pipeline stages. Draw calls and batching. Occlusion culling. Rendering profiling and optimization.

nodes.label: Game UX & Accessibility
nodes.description: Game accessibility features: colorblind modes, remappable controls, subtitle options. Difficulty options. Onboarding and tutorials. UX testing.

nodes.label: Scene Management
nodes.description: Loading and unloading scenes. Scene transitions and loading screens. Additive scene loading. Persistent data across scenes. Scene streaming.

nodes.label: Version Control for Games
nodes.description: Git for game projects. Git LFS for large assets. .gitignore for engine-specific files. Branching strategies for game teams. Perforce basics.

nodes.label: Game Localization
nodes.description: Text localization and string tables. Font support for different scripts. Cultural considerations. Localization testing. Translation workflows.

nodes.label: Game Analytics
nodes.description: Player analytics and telemetry. Heatmaps and session tracking. Funnel analysis for progression. A/B testing in games. Data-driven design decisions.

nodes.label: 2D Lighting & Effects
nodes.description: 2D light sources and shadows. Normal maps for 2D sprites. Glow and bloom effects. Day/night cycles. Fog of war.
